english: one is intent on watering; tshó-ba dka-ṯúb-la (tsho ba dka' thub la) v. dká-ba (dka' ba) compounds; ḵyéd-kyis gsuṅ-ba-la brtén-nas (khyed kyis gsung ba la brten nas) following, obeying (your) orders Glr.; ṅai nús-pa-la rtén-nas (ngai nus pa la rten nas) relying on my strength, i.e. by the help of my strength (you will be able to get to that place) Mil.; hence (b) rtén-nas (rten nas) is frq. used for: in consequence of, with respect to, concerning etc.: rkyen dé-la rtén-nas (rkyen de la rten nas) in consequence of this event (the doctrine spread) Tar. 8, 1; *gha-la tén-nas* (gha la rten nas) why? wherefore? C.; yul ḵyád-par-can-la rtén-nas (yul khyad par can la rten nas) (to sin) with regard to a noble object Thgy.; to hang on, to depend on, to arise or issue from; rtén-par _obrél-ba (rten par 'brel ba) v. rten-_obrél (rten 'brel); to be near, to border on, *tén-te yod* (rten te yod) W. (the two villages) are contiguous to each other; = gtád-pa (gtad pa), stón-pa (ston pa) to be directed, to be situated, to lie towards, lhó-p̱yogs-la (lho phyogs la) to be situated towards the south Sambh.; _od-zér-la rtén-nas gzígs-pa ('od zer la rten nas gzigs pa) to look after or pursue with one's eye a ray of light, like gtód-pa (gtod pa) I. 3. Cf. stén-pa (sten pa). — 2. sbst. that which holds, keeps up, rgyál-poi rtén-pao (rgyal po'i rten pao) (these) are the supports of kings Dzl.; brtén-pa rús-pa'i gnás-lugs bstán-pa (brten pa rus pa'i gnas lugs bstan pa) 'the doctrine of the hold-giving bones', osteology Mng. 3. adj. attached to, faithful C. Comp. rtén-grogs (rten grogs), tshe hril-por grógs-pai rtén-grogs (tshe hril por grogs pa'i rten grogs) perh. erron. for gtán-grogs (gtan grogs). — rtén-gnas (rten gnas) Gram.: the case which denotes the place of a thing or person, the locative. — rten-_obrél (rten 'brel), or in full: rtén-par _obrél-bar gyúr-ba (rten par 'brel bar gyur ba) or _obyúṅ-ba ('byung ba) 'the coming to pass in continuous connection' (the explanation of Burn. I, 623 is grammatically not quite correct) i.e.: 1. in a general sense: the connection between cause and effect; in a special sense, the Buddhist doctrine of the rten-_obrél bcu-gñís (rten 'brel bcu gnyis), निदान, the twelve causes of existence Wdk. 551 (with illustrations); Schl. 23, Burn. I. 485, Köpp. I., 609. 2. the auspices of an undertaking, in as much as the complete knowledge of the causal connection of things implies also a certain prescience of future events; rten-_obrél rtóg-pa (rten 'brel rtog pa) to investigate the auspices, šés-pa (shes pa) to know them, (a physician e.g., when treating a patient, must try to find out the auspices) Med.; rten-_obrél bzaṅ (rten 'brel bzang) or legs (legs) good auspices, ṅan (ngan) bad auspices, frq.; so also frq. col. — rtén-ma (rten ma) prop. support, pillar S.g., *tén-šiṅ* (rten shing) W. a pole used as a prop; rtén-sa (rten sa) Mil.?

the act of arguing, reasoning; dialectics Cs.; Sch. distrust, suspicion (?); ḵa-bšád rtóg-geï slób-dpon (kha bshad rtog ge'i slob dpon) seems to describe a teacher who talks in a hypocritical manner with a mere appearance of wisdom. — rtóg-ge-pa (rtog ge pa) an arguer, disputer, reasoner, dialectician Cs.

I. vb., pf. brtags (brtags) (rtogs (rtogs) q.v.), brtag (brtag), imp. rtog(s) (rtog(s)), 1. to consider, examine, search into, look through, cca (also dat.), brtágs-na mi šes (brtags na mi shes) though one meditates (upon the soul), one cannot understand or fathom it Mil.; frq. with a single or double indirect question: to examine whether (or whether not); brtag-dpyód (brtag dpyod) (or rtóg-zíg (rtog zhig)) gtóṅ-ba (gtong ba) Pth., Mil. id.; brtags-dpyód (brtags dpyod) examination, trial Zam.; c. termin. to discern, to recognize as, e.g. mkhrís-par brtag (mkhris par brtag) it is ascertained to be bile, to be caused by bile Med.; so-sór rtóg-pa (so sor rtog pa) Stg. prob. to recognize as being different. — 2. to muse, to ruminate, to trouble one's head about a thing, which is considered a fault much to be guarded against, and the more so, as religious faith as well as meditation require the mind to be strictly directed and entirely devoted to the one subject in question; hence ma-rtóg tíṅ-_odzin (ma rtog ting 'dzin) Mil. contemplation without any disturbing reflections and by-thoughts; cf. no. II. — 3. v. dog-pa (dog pa). II. sbst. 1. consideration, deliberation, reflection, cf. I., 2; rtóg-pa skyé-ba (rtog pa skye ba), rtóg-pa-la _ojúg-pa (rtog pa la 'jug pa) to reflect on a thing, to indulge in musings Dzl. — 2. scruple, hesitation, rtóg-pa skyés-te (rtog pa skyes te) to grow doubtful, hesitating Mil.; rtog(-pa)-med(-pa) (rtog(-pa)-med(-pa)) simple, unsophisticated; simplicity; singleness of heart. — dé-la rtog-_ojúg mi byed (de la rtog 'jug mi byed) Glr. he does not meddle with that.
